reasoning: The informal statement asserts an improper integral equality. The integrand $\frac{x \sin x}{x^2+a^2}$ is not Lebesgue-integrable on $\mathbb{R}$ (it behaves like $\sin x / x$ at infinity), so the natural reading — and the only one under which the equation is true — is as an improper/principal-value limit. The Lean signature encodes exactly this via `Tendsto (λ y => ∫ x in -y..y, ...) atTop (𝓝 (π·e^{-a}))`. Because the integrand is even ($x \sin x$ is even), the symmetric limit coincides with the full improper integral $\lim_{a\to-\infty, b\to\infty} \int_a^b$, so no mathematical content is lost. The hypothesis `0 < a` matches the informal `a > 0`, and the value $\pi e^{-a}$ is identical. This is a standard faithful encoding of an improper integral in Lean.
